work_order.project_title: Midnight Signals — AI Spooky Short for Teens
work_order.objective:
You are Creative Dev on project 'spooky-teen-shortfilm' (Midnight Signals — AI Spooky Short for Teens). The operator has approved moving from Storyboards v3 into Styleframes v1. Your job: deliver an initial styleframes pack (v1) that locks visual tone before full animatic.

Inputs:
- Reference-grounded Storyboards v3 frames: /home/james/Hermes/state/projects/spooky-teen-shortfilm/storyboards/v3/storyboard_f01.png .. f10.png
- Contact sheet: /home/james/Hermes/state/projects/spooky-teen-shortfilm/storyboards/storyboard_contact_v3.png
- Captions: /home/james/Hermes/state/projects/spooky-teen-shortfilm/storyboards/storyboard_captions_v3.csv
- Reference and acceptance (from project control):
  • Five teens visibly rendered as characters (not placeholders).
  • Mirror the camp reference: tent back-center, lantern left FG, right-side sign, central fire, moonlit forest palette.
  • Lighting: warm fire key + cool moon rim; phones-only darkness reads.
  • PG‑13 tone: suspense over gore.

Deliverables (write to the project root paths):
1) styleframes/styleframes_contact_v1.png (6-up contact sheet)
2) styleframes/styleframes_manifest_v1.csv (frame_id,source,notes)
3) styleframes/styleframes_notes_v1.md (brief: palette, lens/DOF, grain, grade/LUT, layer cues)

Frame targets (align to boards): f02 First strike, f05 All together, f07 Live Photo find, f08 Phones only, f09 Almost ignition, f10 Dawn pocket glow.

Constraints:
- If model image-gen is unavailable, generate a precise styleframes brief + placeholder contact sheet extracted from v3 with annotated overlays to indicate the intended grade/lighting — but clearly label placeholders. Do not claim final styleframes without photographic character render quality.
- Keep filenames and versioning exactly as specified.
- Report blockers explicitly; do not silently skip deliverables.

On completion, print a short DONE summary with created/updated file paths.

delivery_model.strategy_label: Lead -> Creative -> Game Dev -> App Dev
acceptance: Five teens are visibly rendered as characters, not seat markers or abstract geometry.
The boards visibly mirror the supplied camp reference: tent back-center, lantern left foreground, sign on right, central fire, moonlit forest palette.
Lighting reads as warm fire key plus cool moon rim with phones-only darkness where called for.
If the lane can only produce blocking diagrams or placeholder boards, it must report blocked and label the artifact placeholder instead of marking it review-ready.

stage_guidance.operator: Own scope, freeze the delivery target, and keep the handoffs honest.
stage_guidance.creative-dev: Define the creative target, vision guardrails, and artifact acceptance language before implementation drifts.
stage_guidance.app-dev: Own the app, tooling, portal, and review-loop support needed to make the chosen artifact reviewable and repeatable.

handoff_contract: freeze one primary delivery target before downstream implementation expands scope
treat creative-dev as the vision and acceptance guardrail for downstream lanes
treat game-dev as the owner of the playable implementation path once the vision is frozen
treat app-dev as the owner of portal, tooling, packaging, and review-loop support after the build path is clear
require each lane to leave a concrete handoff for the next lane instead of sending the operator back into open-ended review

verification_required: for reference-driven storyboard work, either produce image-grounded frames or explicitly report that the lane is blocked; do not substitute abstract diagrams and call them done
state whether the delivered artifact is a blocking diagram, placeholder board, clean storyboard, or image-grounded redraw
do not mark storyboard work review-ready if the output is still circles, boxes, icons, or other schematic placeholders instead of visibly rendered figures, props, and lighting beats
work_order.project_now: Reference-grounded storyboard pack v3 has been generated from the user-provided campfire image and is ready for review.
work_order.project_next: Review storyboard v3 for shot continuity and choose whether to move into styleframes/animatic assembly.
